description: Customer-facing mobile/web portal for managing alarms, cameras, smart home devices, and security events.

round1.title: Set Your Q3 OKRs
round1.description: Based on the dashboard data, choose 1 objective and 2-3 key results for Q3.

objectives.label: Accelerate User Growth
objectives.description: Focus on acquiring new users and expanding the user base

objectives.label: Stop the Retention Bleed
objectives.description: Address rising churn and declining cohort retention

objectives.label: Fix the Onboarding Funnel
objectives.description: Focus on the massive drop-off between sign-up and active monitoring

objectives.label: Win Enterprise Segment
objectives.description: Focus on commercial/installer segment with low activation and high churn

growth.label: Improve sign-up to activation rate from 22% to 35%

retention.label: Increase NPS from 28 to 40
retention.reason: Lagging indicator, hard to move directly

activation.label: Reduce time-to-first-alarm-test from 14 days to 3 days

round2.title: Design Your Experiment
round2.description: Based on the analytics deep-dive, choose which experiment to run.

options.label: Customer Interviews
options.description: Interview 10 users who dropped off at 'Connect Device' step

options.label: User Survey
options.description: Survey 500 users about their onboarding experience

options.label: A/B Test: Simplified Onboarding
options.description: Test a 3-step onboarding vs current 5-step flow

options.label: A/B Test: Push Notifications
options.description: Test alarm event push notifications vs current email-only

options.label: Prototype Usability Test
options.description: Test a prototype of the new device connection wizard with 5 users

options.label: Feature Flag: Quick Setup Mode
options.description: Build and roll out a 'quick setup' mode to 10% of new users

round3.title: Ship, Iterate, or Kill
round3.description: Review your experiment results and decide what to do next.

findings: 7/10 users found device pairing confusing — expected it to be automatic via QR code
5/10 said they gave up after 2 failed Bluetooth pairing attempts
Most mentioned they'd expected a guided video walkthrough
interview.recommendation: The core problem is device pairing UX, not the number of steps

findings: Top pain point: 'Connecting my device' (68% selected)
NPS of onboarding flow: -12 (vs product NPS of +28)
survey.recommendation: Clear signal that device connection is the primary friction point

ab_push.summary: A/B Test: Push Notifications for Alarm Events

prototype.summary: Usability Test: New Device Connection Wizard
findings: 4/5 users completed the new flow without assistance (vs 2/5 with current flow)
Average time to connect: 4.2 min (vs 11.8 min current)
All users understood QR code scanning immediately
1 user confused by 'zone assignment' step — suggested auto-detect
prototype.recommendation: Strong usability signal. QR-based pairing is a clear winner. Zone auto-detect worth exploring.

feature_flag.summary: Feature Flag: Quick Setup Mode (10% rollout)
feature_flag.note: Quick setup skips zone configuration — may cause more false alarms

vanity.explanation: Downloads don't measure value — most users never activate
Cohort retention directly measures whether users find ongoing value

vanity.metric: Number of features shipped this quarter
vanity.explanation: Output metric — shipping features doesn't guarantee outcomes
Measures activation speed — directly tied to user success
Cumulative counter that only goes up — tells you nothing about current health

vanity.metric: Monthly active users who triggered 3+ events
vanity.explanation: Measures meaningful engagement, not just logins
Lagging, biased (only motivated users rate), and not actionable
Shows exactly where users drop off — directly drives improvement
Can be a leading indicator of UX problems — actionable when segmented
No correlation with product value or business outcomes
Shows where value is concentrated — drives strategic decisions
Activity ≠ value. A confused user generates lots of page views too

pvalue.explanation: p=0.018 < 0.05 threshold. With ~3,200 total users, this is a reliable result.
p=0.23 — the small lift could easily be noise. Sample size too small for this effect size.
p=0.041 — just under the 0.05 threshold. With 10K+ users, the small absolute lift is reliable.
p=0.08 — promising direction but underpowered. Need 2-3x more sample to confirm.
p=0.52 — no effect detected. The redesign didn't change behavior (and that's okay to learn).
p=0.001 — highly significant. Large effect (+9.5pp) with adequate sample. Ship it.
